overview: Activity on July 3-4 centered on two patch series. Avinash Duduskar submitted a v6 update adding bidirectional VLAN support to the bpf_fib_lookup() helper, introducing BPF_FIB_LOOKUP_VLAN and BPF_FIB_LOOKUP_VLAN_INPUT flags for handling VLAN-tagged traffic on both egress and ingress paths. Maxim Khmelevskii contributed a correctness fix to the s390 BPF JIT by replacing the ly instruction with llgf to avoid unintended sign-extension when loading 32-bit values.

patches.title: bpf: Add BPF_FIB_LOOKUP_VLAN flag to bpf_fib_lookup() helper
patches.author: Avinash Duduskar
patches.summary: This patch (part of the v6 bidirectional VLAN support series) adds a new BPF_FIB_LOOKUP_VLAN flag to the bpf_fib_lookup() helper, allowing BPF programs to supply VLAN context when performing FIB lookups. This enables the kernel routing logic to make accurate egress path decisions for VLAN-tagged packets from within a BPF program. The change fills a gap for XDP and tc programs operating in VLAN-aware network environments. Being in its sixth revision, the patch has undergone substantial review and refinement before submission.

patches.title: bpf: Add BPF_FIB_LOOKUP_VLAN_INPUT flag to bpf_fib_lookup() helper
patches.author: Avinash Duduskar
patches.summary: This companion patch in the VLAN series introduces BPF_FIB_LOOKUP_VLAN_INPUT, which extends bpf_fib_lookup() to account for VLAN tags on the ingress side of a lookup. Together with BPF_FIB_LOOKUP_VLAN, it completes bidirectional VLAN support, giving BPF programs full control over FIB lookups in environments with tagged traffic flowing in both directions. This is valuable for BPF-based virtual switching and routing stacks that must correctly handle 802.1Q VLAN headers.

patches.title: s390/bpf: Replace ly instruction with llgf
patches.author: Maxim Khmelevskii
patches.summary: This patch corrects a subtle JIT code-generation bug on s390 by substituting the ly (Load) instruction with llgf (Load Logical Long from Fullword) when loading 32-bit values into 64-bit registers. The ly instruction performs sign-extension, which incorrectly fills the upper 32 bits of a register when the source value's high bit is set, causing wrong results for unsigned 32-bit operations in BPF programs. Switching to llgf ensures zero-extension, matching the BPF semantics for 32-bit loads. A corresponding selftest for bpf_get_smp_processor_id is included to verify correct behavior on s390.
